twitter_thread: Only 12% of software engineers can write deployable code on their first try, with 60% needing 3+ iterations.
40% of top engineers use Excel for project management, despite having tools like Git, Jira, and Asana.
Over-reliance on React or Angular without knowing JavaScript basics is a common mistake, causing 30% of project delays.
Contributing to open-source projects like Linux (23k contributors) or Node.js (1.3k contributors) improves coding skills.
Debugging with Chrome DevTools resolves 80% of issues, but 20% of engineers don't use it, relying on console logs only.
What's the most effective way you've found to debug complex code issues? #softwareengineering #coding
Experienced engineers use design patterns like Singleton (15% usage) or Factory (20% usage) for efficient code.
